chapter.label: Tale 81
chapter.title: A well that filled with WATER

paragraphs.text: WHEN AN ICON OF THE SAME neither get the vessel to (he sea nor even move it from the spot ABBA THEODOSIOS WAS LET DOWN INTO IT where it lay; for the vessel was under the <spell of> men who were workers of evil. The owner of the ship was very disturbed and at a The same fathers also told us that in those days, a Christ-loving loss what to do next. By the providence of God, it happened that woman of the district of Apamea dug a well. She spent a great deal the elder came that way. When the ship-owner saw the elder, of money on the project and dug very deep, but she found no water. becanse he had some knowledge of his qualities, he said to him: Having put so much money and effort into the project, she was very ‘Abba, please pray for my ship, sir. On account of enchantment, it discouraged. Then one day, she had a vision of somebady saying to ; cannot be launched”. The elder said to the shipmaster: ‘Go, give me her: ‘Send for and bring the picture of Abba Theodosios at something to eat and God will come to your aid’. The elder said Skopelos and by that means God will give you water". The woman this so that the ship-owner would go away to his own house. The sent two men at once. They took the icon of the saint and Jet it monk approached the ship alone, made three prostration before down into the well and immediately water began to flow; it filled the God and three times he signed the vessel with the sign of the cross, well-shaft up to the half-way poiat. The men who drew the icon up in the name of our lord Jesus Christ. The elder then came to the out of the water brought us some of it; we drank of it and all gave i house of the ship-owner and said to him: ‘Go now and Jaunch your thanks to God. vessel’.
